video_title: Top 20 Craziest Pizzas on #DDD w/ Guy Fieri (Extended) | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: Aubrey Jade's Pizza Garage
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm on the northern side of Boulder, Colorado, to check out a pizza joint. Oh, you want more? Oh, no, it's just not any pizza joint. No, no, this chef is studying Italy. She's made pizza for the greats on the East and West Coast. And from what I'm told, you better watch out. This is Aubrey Jade's Pizza Garage.

dishes.description: Homemade dough with high-gluten flour and mother dough. Topped with homemade hand-pinched sausage, California tomato sauce, mozzarella, natural casing pepperoni, roasted jalapenos, chopped garlic, and olive oil. Cooked in a 650F oven and finished with oregano.
dishes.guy_response: The crust is outstanding. When you're going to the extent that you're going to here, starting with the mother or the biga, it just shows. The sausage is great. The Sicilian oregano is dynamite. The right size pepperoni. The roasted jalapeno is just giving a little bit of that heat. Mm. I can't find anything I don't like. The crust on that, it's a meal in itself. That's why you start with the dough, huh? Just the right level. It's full of spice. You can taste that fat from the meat. It's really good.

restaurants.name: Wild Barley
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in the Alamo Heights area of San Antonio, Texas. And what is that they say about Texas? It's always bigger in Texas. Well, I hear about these two guys, and they got a joint where they're making everything big. Let's see, they're making their beer. They're making their bread. They're making their pizza dough. This is wild barley.

dishes.description: Sourdough pizza dough topped with house-made barbecue sauce, a white cheese blend, diced brisket, pickled jalapenos, pickled red onions, and cheddar cheese. Cooked at 600F and finished with more barbecue sauce.
dishes.guy_response: That pizza's exciting. It's spicy. It's rich. It's tangy. The dough is crunchy and super flavorful. Love the barbecue sauce finish. One of the things I love the most about it is the jalapeno and the pickled onion. Every bite has got a little different thing, depending on where you're biting into it. That is a benchmark pizza right there.

restaurants.name: Outrigger Pizza
restaurants.guy_intro: Now I'm over here in Maui. Hit it, kid. To check out a trailer that the guy made himself, and he's also scratch-baking the dough by hand. This is Outrigger Pizza.

dishes.description: Wood-fired pizza with a garlic aioli base, topped with dry-rubbed and braised pulled pork, Maui sweet onions, sweet bell peppers, mozzarella, and Romano cheese. Cooked in a 700F wood-fired oven for 90 seconds and finished with fresh Parmesan and lilikoi glaze.
dishes.guy_response: It's dynamite. The crust is too legit to quit. Not too much cheese. Nothing's overdone. Right. When you do get the little bite of pork, you love it. When you get a little bit of the pepper, you're down with it. Mm. Really dynamite pizza.

restaurants.name: Bonfire
restaurants.guy_intro: Well, let me introduce you to Bonfire. Walking in, I have a Gila Monster burger. It's not your typical restaurant. Arizona Gold Wings in the window. Initially, we were like, oh, it's a new sports bar. Let's go watch the game. But the food is fantastic.

dishes.description: House-made pizza dough topped with mozzarella, smoked pork, smoked chicken, smoked brisket, and caramelized onions. Cooked at 650F and finished with garlic butter, oregano, white cheddar cheese curds, pecorino romano, and shredded parmesan.
dishes.guy_response: In all honesty, when we started putting all those ingredients on the pizza, I thought it was going to weigh down that crust. Mm-hmm. But the way you cooked that pizza, the way the crust is house made, kept its texture, the structure, and the sauce works. That's a good balance. Really good pie. I'm not finding it in that. Well done.

restaurants.name: Armory Square Pizzeria
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here in Armory Square in Syracuse, New York. You see that joint right there? Shot Triple D there. It's called Kitty Hoyne's. It's like the real deal Irish pub. And now, I'm directly across the street. I mean, this is kind of like a Triple D double dip. This place here, I hear they make their own sauce, they make their own bread, and they make their own pasta. I mean, the pasta-bilities are endless.

dishes.description: Flatbread pizza topped with garlic oil, fresh mozzarella, dried cherries, caramelized onions, Humboldt Fog goat cheese, and house-cured and smoked duck bacon. Cooked and finished with pomegranate syrup, cracked pepper, basil, and parsley.
dishes.guy_response: The crust on this flatbread is righteous. So the bread has such great hue. The fresh moths, tartness of that cherry, a little bit of funkaliciousness coming from that Humboldt Fog. Nobody takes the lead role except the duck bacon. It's probably one of the best duck dishes I've ever had. Yeah, you jackpotted on that one, girl.

restaurants.name: Weights and Measures
restaurants.guy_intro: Houston's known for a lot. Great rodeo, amazing barbecue, really good sports teams. You know what they should get known for is this restaurant scene, like this joint, Weights and Measures.

dishes.description: House-made pizza dough topped with French soubise, Gruyere cheese, roasted carrots (with orange zest and honey), and Fresno pepper. Cooked in a wood-fired oven and finished with Moroccan dukkah spice.
dishes.guy_response: Outstanding. The tang of the Gruyere. The sweetness of the caramelized carrot. The spice of the Fresno. Without the duca, you sprinkle on like pixie dust. Wouldn't be the same. It's giving me goosebumps. That is probably in the top three most unique pizzas I've ever had in my life.

restaurants.name: Grace and Hammer Pizzeria
restaurants.guy_intro: Well, anyhow, I'm here in Redmond, Oregon, right outside of Bend. Where they're serving pizza at the pulpit. Listen. Can you hear it? That might be the Bells of Flavortown Church. This is Grace and Hammer Pizzeria.

dishes.description: House-made sourdough pizza dough topped with kalbi sauce, fresh mozzarella, 10-hour smoked gochujang pork, and fontina cheese. Cooked in a 700-800F wood-fired oven and finished with pineapple slaw and pickled onion.
dishes.guy_response: It's dynamite. That crust is so light and airy and fluffy. The balance that you create with that pineapple slaw, because you're getting a lot of heat from the serranos and the jalapenos, with the acidity from the onions countering the brown sugar that you have in the kalbi sauce, that's what gives it the balance and the harmony. I know I'm preaching to the choir on this, but I got to confess, it's excellent pizza.

restaurants.name: Red Wagon Pizza
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Minneapolis, Minnesota. Now, we get recommendations for Triple D joints from a lot of people. But in particular, when your crew that shoots Triple D, hold on, this crew right here, when they tell you that you got to come check out a pizza joint that's right down the street from their house, well, you know I'm there. This is Red Wagon Pizza.

dishes.description: Handmade dough with heritage wheat flour and day-old mother dough. Topped with house-made sauce, a blend of mozzarella and provolone, house-made sausage, banana peppers, and sopressata. Cooked for 90 seconds and finished with crushed red pepper, garlic oil, and fig-infused balsamic.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, dynamite. Everything you're doing in the pizza is balanced. Fresh-made sausage. You've got handmade dough. You've got homemade sauce. You've got a nice little acid bite in there coming from the banana pepper. A little touch of the chili flake. It's an artisan pizza, man. Everybody at home right now is going, oh, I want a bite of that pizza, because it's the real deal. Fantastic.

restaurants.name: Hungry Robot
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here in Fairbanks, Alaska. It's been a while since we were up here. I always find an eclectic mix of food in this area. So what am I up to try? A pizza joint. No, just any average pizza joint. You see, I'm a big pizza guy. I love pizza. I love pizza. I love pizza. You see, he makes the pizzas inside, but then he's got a hole in the wall and cooks in the wood-fired ovens outside. In Alaska weather? Are you kidding me? This is Hungry Robot.

dishes.description: Wood-fired pizza with a roasted garlic white sauce base, topped with mozzarella, pickles, and house-made sweet Italian sausage. Cooked in a birch wood-fired oven and finished with a dilly shaker (Parmesan, Romano, dill) and dilly drizzle (ranch gone wild).
dishes.guy_response: It's delicious. The crust is about as spot on as you get. Light, full of pockets, but it's got good chew. It's tender. I like biting into it and getting the pickle crunch. I like the fresh acidity contrasting to the creaminess of the base spread. And then you get the crumble from the sausage. And I think the sprinkle is dynamite. That's a destination pizza.

restaurants.name: Griggs Street Pizza
restaurants.guy_intro: So that's my introduction to Greenwich, Connecticut. Someone hits the front end of the Camaro. Out of control, dude. I'm maniacs. This place better be worth it. What's the story? My boy Randy is making incredible pies. They're making their own bread. They're pulling cheese every day. I love it. I got the evidence. I found it. Hunter, put the evidence in the car. Do you want me to get the police report? Hunter, put the evidence in the car. Where's witnesses? I can get the witnesses. Put the evidence in the car. This is Griggs Street Pizza.

dishes.description: Sourdough pizza dough topped with house-made tomato sauce, fresh mozzarella, ripped basil, pepperoni, and garlic oil. Cooked at 650F and finished with Sicilian oregano, pecorino, and house-made hot honey (roasted habaneros and garlic).
dishes.guy_response: That's the one. The crust is outstanding. It's light. It's airy. It's got good sourdough, good yeast to it. This simple tomato sauce where tomato is alive and well. A-plus pizza. A-plus, A-plus. It hits all the marks.

restaurants.name: Little Betty at Wiseacre Brewing
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here in Memphis, Tennessee on B.B. King Boulevard, not too far from downtown. And I got to tell you, when I pulled up, the first thing I thought is, this is like strange brew. I mean, here is this gigantic beer brewing factory. And this thing is huge. And I hear inside, they got a little pizza joint that is scratch-making just about everything. I'm hungry, and I want a beer. This is Little Betty at Wiseacre Brewing.

dishes.description: Pizza dough made with beer wort, topped with ricotta cream, mozzarella, and mortadella. Cooked at 550F and finished with stracciatella cheese, pistachio crunch, pecorino, and black pepper honey.
dishes.guy_response: The saltiness of the mortadella, the ricotta, nice and creamy but not too much. But once you get to this crumble, the pistachios, that's where it gets crazy. Everything's fine. And what do you do? You drop the hammer and take me to black pepper honey. Bro. It's a different dimension of pizza. The only thing this pizza could be is this trippy. Dynamite pie.

restaurants.name: Restaurant 415
restaurants.owner_chef: Andre Mouton, Amelia Mouton, Seth Baker and Sean Olander
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Fort Collins, Colorado, about 60 minutes from Denver and here at the home of Colorado State University. Now, I'm here to check out a joint that's got a lot of character. Let's see. This team of four worked together on one restaurant and then they took off to open their own joint that they like to call your neighborhood eatery. If you're looking for a good time, this is your place. This is Restaurant 415.

dishes.description: House-made pizza dough topped with olive oil, fresh garlic, raw sliced tomatillos, chopped garbanzo beans, cumin seed, cheddar cheese, and fresh jalapenos. Cooked for 8 minutes and finished with fresh cilantro.
dishes.guy_response: Never in a million years would I have thought of taking a tomatillo, slicing it thin like that, and letting it cook off in eight minutes. I love the bite of the tomatillo. The acidity, the freshness, it's still a little bit of crunch. Jalapeno's great. The garlic's not overwhelming. Meagerness of the garbanzo, I'm telling you, that's a spot on pizza.

restaurants.name: Blackbird Wood Fire
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Fargo, North Dakota. And if you haven't been here, you should come and check it out. You know, things are a little different up here. Let's see, the weather, well, it could change dramatically at the drop of a hat. And the food scene, it will surprise you. You know, folks up here kind of go to the beat of their own drum. Kind of like the joint we're going to check out now. Six and a half years of making wood-fired pizzas their way. This is Blackbird Wood Fire.

dishes.description: Unconventional pizza dough topped with house-made bechamel sauce, Parmesan, house-made sausage, apples, and sage. Finished with snap pea micros.
dishes.guy_response: That is one delicious pizza. Great chew to the crust. Get a nice crackle on the outside with true gluten. Gluten-style flour pull. And this little Bechamel Parmesan cheese program works completely right. Sausage still has good fat to it. A lot of great apple. The key at the end, though, that fresh herb on top. I can just go on and on about it. Outstanding.

restaurants.name: Mickey G's Pizzeria
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here at Jackson, Wyoming, about a half block off the town square, right in front of the Pink Garter Theater. Back in the day, this was the theater that you came to to catch a musical or a play. Now it's where you come to see some rockin' bands. And speaking of rockin', locals around here tell me you want some rockin' pizza, you got to go to the joint right under the theater. This is Mickey G's Pizzeria.

dishes.description: Hand-tossed pizza dough topped with fresh basil pesto, mozzarella cheese, 24-hour marinated and roasted chicken, artichoke hearts, red onions, and ricotta cheese. Cooked at 575F.
dishes.guy_response: Roasting the chicken with the garlic, outrageous. Making your own pesto over the top. Little bit of that ricotta coming in, creaminess, saltiness.

restaurants.name: Intermezzo Pizzeria
restaurants.owner_chef: Georgie Avramovic, Bronco Avramovic and Dobrila Avramovic
restaurants.guy_intro: You know, we've all heard the term, live in the American dream. Well, I'm here in Charlotte, North Carolina, a mile from the downtown area. We're a Serbian family. Our family is living the American dream. They got a funky little joint where they're serving up, now get this, pizza and Serbian food, just what you'd expect. This is Intermezzo Pizzeria.

dishes.description: A calzone-like dish with pizza dough filled with mozzarella, ricotta, parsley, garlic, mushrooms, and ham. Cooked at 505F and served with sour cream, provolone cheese, and house-made pizza sauce on top.
dishes.guy_response: Wow, that's really, really good. The yeast in the dough really adds a nice tang. The sour cream, without question, is fantastic. And then the mushrooms and the ham, they're dynamite. I'm actually disappointed that I haven't tried this before. You learn something new every day. Pirozhka. Pirozhka. Outstanding.

restaurants.name: The Saucy Sicilian
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here in OKC for a little pizza in the parking lot. Now, this isn't just a regular old pizza truck. No, this thing is a monster. And the oven, not any regular old oven. No, it's wood-fired. And the recipes, well, they're not your everyday recipes, because they're handed down from generations. This is the Saucy Sicilian.

dishes.description: Wood-fired pizza with a 3-day proofed dough, topped with ricotta cheese, mozzarella, fresh figs, roasted garlic, and roasted red pepper. Cooked in a pecan hickory oven and finished with prosciutto and balsamic maple glaze.
dishes.guy_response: I mean, it's just really delicious. The crust is the foundation, and it's out of bounds. Neapolitan pizza has to be in balance. And you give good portions, a good amount of prosciutto. It's an honest pizza. Scratch made, handmade, with a lot of family love in it. Well done.

dishes.description: Homemade meatballs (50-50 pork and beef) roasted and then simmered for 8 hours in a marinara sauce with seared short rib, garlic, onions, tomato paste, tomato sauce, sugar, red pepper flake, and bay leaves. Served with Parmesan and fresh basil.
dishes.guy_response: Delicious and tender and moist. Great sauce. A little sprinkle of the Parmesan right at the end. That fresh basil. Hits that sauce and just blooms. And that's a good thing. That's a good amount of garlic. I'm telling you, your grandma taught you right. Great job, chef.

restaurants.name: Junkyard
restaurants.guy_intro: OK, I've got a party in your mouth. And a fried cheese pizza. Today, I'm having the fried cheese pizza. The best part is the cheese. And that's what the crust is.

dishes.description: A crustless pizza made by grilling Parmesan, cheddar, onion, gorgonzola, pepperoni, and mozzarella. Topped with homemade pizza sauce, olive oil, grilled pineapple, and in-house pesto. Cooked under a dome and finished with sliced tomatoes.
dishes.guy_response: This right here is just a gigantic thing of that crunchy fried cheese that you love on the outside of a quesadilla. The fact that you put toppings on it, it's crazy. Fried cheese, can't beat it.

restaurants.name: Sal's Gilbert Pizza
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Gilbert, Arizona, about 30 miles from Phoenix, and I love coming down here. I get to rip around in the desert in my Razor. There's great food, really cool people. I like the story of this place. Let's see, the dude is from Sicily, Italy. He moves to New York, meets his wife, has a family, and then they all move here to Gilbert to open a pizza joint. That's right, this is Sal's Gilbert Pizza.

dishes.description: New York style pizza dough, par-baked, then topped with whole milk mozzarella, ziti pasta cooked in marinara sauce with cream, seasoning, butter, Parmesan, and parsley. Finished with more mozzarella and Parmesan.
dishes.guy_response: Dough is nice and crispy. I love pasta. I love pizza. But the key is pasta's got to be al dente. Yes. And the dough has to be crispy. That right there, that's the optimum bite. If I could come in and get this. I'd be here two or three times a week. Nice job, man.

restaurants.name: Dirty Water Dough Company
restaurants.guy_intro: We're going to check out a funky joint that is using beer to make their pizza dough. And I hear it's the real deal. This is Dirty Water Dough Company.

dishes.description: Pizza dough made with special milled wheat flour and Dirty Water IPA. Topped with a light red sauce, shredded mozzarella, yellow cheddar, and house-made taco meat (ground beef with paprika and enchilada sauce). Finished with lettuce, diced tomato, nacho cheese chips, and a sour cream swirl.
dishes.guy_response: The crust is dynamite. It's toothsome. Got good pull. I get the yeastiness out of it. It's delicious. I mean, as you can tell, I didn't like it at all. My son Ryder, he's going to lose his mind on this one.

restaurants.name: Pizzeria Lola
restaurants.guy_intro: They have one of the only copper-covered, wood-fired, French pizza ovens, a Le Pagnole, at this place, where they're doing off-the-hook pizza. This is Pizzeria Lola.

dishes.description: 48-hour fermented pizza dough topped with house red sauce, Wisconsin mozzarella, house-made fennel sausage (Berkshire pork), and natural casing pepperoni. Cooked in a wood-fired oven (550-600F).
dishes.guy_response: We got this heirloom pork with the real fat. And the real flavor. And the fennel is rocking this. A lot of people say, oh, sausage and pepperoni. I've had that all the time. This is way beyond your everyday pizza joint on the corner, sausage and pepperoni. You got to try this one. It's the most heightened, delicious pepperoni sausage pizza you've ever had. You're like, this is what pepperoni is supposed to taste like.
